show OF resistance to reductions
of wages in the West, forced the
Hill system to abandon it's policy
of cutting wages sufficiently to
make up the additional expense OF
hiring extra operators.


The Pennsylvania, Baltimore q
Ohio, Illinois Central, C. B. & O.,
Milwaukee, Wisconsin Central,
Santa Fe and Southern Pacific are
among the big lines calling for
more operators in order to comply
with the law.


A canvass of the operators vote
in st. Paul last night showed prac-
tically the same result as that
voted in Spokane-a unanimous
stand against reduction. The
company asked for another confer.
ence, the result Of which is that
the operators get the shorter hours
without battle.


There were ~ railroad represent
tatives before the interstate com.
merce commission yesterday ready
to offer further argument In favor
of extending the date of putting irl
effect the nine hour law, but the
Northern Pacific's action indicates
that their efforts were unavailing1


The meeting of Spokane railway
telegraphers of the Pasco and Idaho
divisions at the Pedicord hotel lase
night was attended by Al delegatesk
all representing solid sentiment
against accepting the reduction. AS
result of the meeting local.
lodge of the railway telegrapherS.
